name: Mock strategy for external payment service
description: Evaluate the skill's ability to design a mocking strategy for an external payment gateway in a checkout flow, ensuring isolation from the real service while testing all response scenarios.
prompt: I have an OrderService that calls a PaymentGateway.charge(amount, currency, token) method. The gateway is an external HTTP service. Design a unit test strategy with mocking for: successful charge, declined card, network timeout, and idempotency key conflict. Show me the test structure and mock setup.

assertions.description: Should recommend stubs or test doubles for the payment gateway rather than calling the real service
Should include a test case for network timeout handling as an error path
Should include specific assertions for each test scenario outcome
Should structure tests using the Arrange-Act-Assert pattern or equivalent
Should address the idempotency conflict scenario as an edge case

name: TDD red-green-refactor cycle for a validator
description: Evaluate the skill's ability to walk through a complete TDD red-green-refactor cycle for implementing an email validator, demonstrating each phase with concrete code.
prompt: Walk me through a TDD red-green-refactor cycle to implement an EmailValidator.validate(email) function. It should accept standard emails, reject emails without @, reject emails with spaces, and handle edge cases like consecutive dots. Show each phase clearly.

assertions.description: Should explicitly identify the Red phase where a failing test is written first
Should explicitly identify the Green phase where minimal code is written to pass
Should explicitly identify the Refactor phase where code is cleaned up under green tests
Should show the test failing before implementation as proof of test validity
Should address edge cases like consecutive dots or unusual valid formats
